top_categories.workout_context: 154 miles. Your legs will beg to quit after 120. This trains the power output you need from mile 130 onward.
Edition Zero's New Zealand mountains, South Island gravel forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 154 miles.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Edition Zero's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before November.
75ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 154 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
At Edition Zero, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
154 miles rewards fat oxidation. This volume work shifts your fuel mix so you're still burning fat at hour 5, not bonking at hour 3.
11,500ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
11,500ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Edition Zero. Train it until it feels boring.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 11,500ft of climbing on new Zealand mountain gravel does to your power file on race day.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Edition Zero, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.

race_overlay.nutrition: Ultra-distance fueling for 154 miles: 80–100g carbs/hour from mile 1 — don’t wait until you’re hungry. Practice your exact race-day nutrition in every long training ride. Carry backup calories. 154 miles burns 8,000–12,000+ calories — you cannot replace them all, but you must try.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at Edition Zero. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at Edition Zero requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on new zealand mountain gravel to build confidence. Expect: new zealand mountains, south island gravel, regional event.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, Gravel Specific, and Race Simulation to prepare you for 154 miles of new zealand mountain gravel in Waimate, New Zealand.
